generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard LiDAR sensor providing high-resolution 3D spatial data for environmental perception in autonomous vehicle applications. Part of the sensing infrastructure for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) with raw point cloud output."
]

instance IntelRealSenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Depth camera providing complementary depth information alongside LiDAR data for environmental perception. Used for object detection and tracking in ACC applications."
]

instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingSW : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Software component responsible for preprocessing raw LiDAR point clouds into structured object tracks and vehicle state information. Implements clustering and tracking algorithms for ACC-relevant data."
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance RawLiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Unstructured 3D spatial points collected from the Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks and vehicle state information generated by the Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking software component from raw LiDAR data."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingSW
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Depth information collected from the Intel RealSense D435i camera for environmental perception."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelRealSenseD435iDepthCamera
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Aggregated vehicle dynamics including speed, acceleration, and trajectory derived from sensor fusion and actuation feedback."
]

instance ActuationFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Motor, steering, and servo actuation states (e.g., motor RPM, steering angle, servo position) transmitted from physical actuators to the Digital Twin Entity."
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPathData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Environmental data including object tracks and projected driving paths used for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) logic."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARPointCloudModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric model representing raw LiDAR point cloud data in 3D space for environmental perception."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model converting raw LiDAR point clouds into structured object tracks and vehicle state information for ACC applications."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model aggregating vehicle dynamics (speed, acceleration, trajectory) from sensor fusion and actuation feedback."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance RawLiDARPointCloudDataInstance : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unstructured 3D spatial points collected from the Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor for environmental perception."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawLiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracksDataInstance : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks and vehicle state information generated by the Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking software component."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedObjectTracksData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance DepthCameraDataInstance : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Depth information collected from the Intel RealSense D435i camera for environmental perception."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateDataInstance : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated vehicle dynamics including speed, acceleration, and trajectory derived from sensor fusion and actuation feedback."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance ActuationFeedbackDataInstance : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor, steering, and servo actuation states transmitted from physical actuators to the Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationFeedbackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPathDataInstance : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Environmental data including object tracks and projected driving paths used for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) logic."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProjectedDrivingPathData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataPreProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component responsible for preprocessing raw sensor data (e.g., noise filtering, coordinate alignment, clustering, and tracking) to generate structured data for ACC applications."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware layer ensuring serialized data alignment, unit conversions, and message type standardization for all data streams between physical and virtual components."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance ROS2CommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 (ROS2) network enabling intra-entity communication for software component interactions within the Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance UDPCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi network using UDP protocol for real-time physical-to-digital data transmission with low-latency requirements (100ms)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance VisualizationPreProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component transforming raw data into 3D visualizations for real-time monitoring and reporting of vehicle state data."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization, logging, and reporting of ACC-relevant data with low end-to-end latency (100ms) for safety-critical operations. Targets operators for runtime monitoring and developers for troubleshooting."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateInsight, HistoricalDataReportingInsight, LowLatencyCommunicationAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Distributed computation and control logic delegation offloading the entire ACC system from resource-constrained ECUs to external infrastructure (e.g., cloud or edge). Enables lead vehicle detection, desired speed calculation, and actuation commanding."
    DTDFVocab:provides LeadVehicleDetectionInsight, DesiredSpeedCalculationInsight, ActuationCommandingAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Teleoperation and runtime control of ACC enabling remote enabling/disabling, parameter tuning, and emergency interventions. Supports geographical decoupling for remote development, testing, and operation."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCControlInsight, ParameterTuningInsight, EmergencyInterventionAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time display of vehicle state data including speed, acceleration, sensor inputs, projected driving path, and detected object tracks in proximity for ACC monitoring."
]

instance HistoricalDataReportingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Saved and generated reports of historical vehicle state data for post-mortem analysis and troubleshooting."
]

instance LowLatencyCommunicationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Ensures low end-to-end latency communication (100ms) between the physical twin and Digital Twin System for safety-critical ACC operations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LeadVehicleDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of preceding vehicles using sensor data (radar, camera, LiDAR) for ACC logic."
]

instance DesiredSpeedCalculationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computation of optimal speed based on distance-to-lead-vehicle and safety margins for ACC applications."
]

instance ActuationCommandingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Sends desired speed commands back to the physical twin for execution (e.g., throttle/brake adjustments) in ACC systems."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ACCControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Remote enabling/disabling of ACC functionality via UI commands for field deployment and testing."
]

instance ParameterTuningInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Remote adjustment of ACC parameters such as desired speed, time-headway, and acceleration limits."
]

instance EmergencyInterventionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote emergency braking commands to override on-board logic in safety-critical ACC scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic Speed Controller (ESC) responsible for managing motor speed and actuation commands for the autonomous vehicle's Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) system."
]

instance SteeringServo : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering servo component executing steering angle adjustments based on ACC control commands for autonomous driving."
]

// C10: Additional Models/Data (Physical Twin Entity)
instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Software model implementing clustering and tracking algorithms for converting raw LiDAR point clouds into structured object tracks and vehicle state information."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

// C11: Additional Enablers (Physical Twin Entity)
instance DataCollectionAndDeviceControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Entity responsible for interfacing with physical sensors and actuators, collecting operational data, and managing device communication for the Physical Twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

// C17: Additional Actions (Physical Twin Entity)
instance VehicleStateAggregationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Aggregation of motor, steering, and servo actuation states into comprehensive vehicle state representations for ACC applications."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataTranslationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Ensures serialized data alignment, unit conversions, and message type standardization for all data streams between physical and virtual components."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) is implemented through the following control flows:

1. **Command Types**:
   - **Actuation Commands**: Motor, steering, and servo control signals generated by the *Actuation FE* (e.g., throttle adjustments, steering angle adjustments).
   - **ACC Control Signals**: Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) commands derived from the *Controlling FE*, including path corrections and speed adjustments.
   - **User Inputs**: Control panel inputs (e.g., manual overrides, mode selections) translated via the *User Interface FE*.

2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - **Structured ROS2 Messages**: Commands are serialized into ROS2 message formats (e.g., `std_msgs/Float32` for steering angles, `geometry_msgs/Twist` for motor actuation).
   - **Data Translation Layer**: Ensures command formats are compatible between the Digital Twin Entity and Physical Twin Entity (e.g., unit conversions, message type alignment).

3. **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
   - **Not explicitly detailed in the document**, but implied via the *Data Translation* FE’s role in ensuring data integrity and consistency.

4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - **Actuation Feedback Loop**: Physical twin entities (e.g., motor, steering servo) return state updates (e.g., actual RPM, steering position) to the Digital Twin for validation.
   - **Visual Confirmation**: 3D visualization (Fig. 3a) reflects real-time physical state, enabling operator verification of command execution.

5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - **ACC Overrides**: Conflicts between manual user inputs (via *User Interface FE*) and ACC-generated commands are resolved via priority rules (not specified in the document).
   - **Data Translation Arbitration**: Ensures no conflicting commands are propagated to the physical twin (e.g., by validating message consistency).

6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - **Implicit via Feedback**: Physical twin state updates (e.g., motor RPM, steering angle) are looped back to the Digital Twin for comparison against commanded values.
   - **Visualization Alignment**: The 3D model’s state must match the physical twin’s state (e.g., vehicle trajectory in Fig. 3a aligns with LiDAR-derived paths).
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (often mandated by standards) is required. The system leverages **time synchronization protocols** (unspecified but implied to be ROS2-compatible) to align distributed components, ensuring deterministic behavior for real-time services such as sensor data processing, actuation, and device communication. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the 100 ms synchronization window, dictating the maximum allowable delay for data exchange between the Physical Twin Entity (sensors/actuators) and Digital Twin Entity (data management/control)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS follows an **ISO 23247 RA-inspired, entity-centric orchestration pattern** with the following key coordination mechanisms:
   - **Functional Entity (FE) Mapping**: Each FE (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*, *3D Visualization*) is implemented as an **individual ROS2 package**, encapsulating one or more ROS2 nodes. This modularity enables **dynamic resource allocation** and fault isolation, as packages can be deployed on distinct hardware components (e.g., edge devices, cloud).
   - **Entity-Based Decomposition**: The system deviates from ISO 23247 by **expanding the Physical Twin Entity’s scope** to include general automotive functionality (beyond resource-specific OME roles), which may require **cross-entity ownership negotiations** during engineering. The Digital Twin Entity acts as the **central orchestrator** for data flows between the Physical Twin (sensors/actuators) and User Entity (visualization/control).
   - **Inter-Entity Communication**: Coordination relies on **ROS2’s publish-subscribe and service models**, with implicit synchronization via the 100 ms clock accuracy. The *User Interface FE*’s split into visualization and control panels introduces **asynchronous coordination** between user-facing components, potentially using ROS2 topics for real-time updates.
   - **Resource Management**: The Physical Twin Entity’s hardware-specific deployment (e.g., compute, sensors) implies **heterogeneous resource orchestration**, where the DTS must manage priorities for latency-sensitive services (e.g., actuation) versus batch-processing tasks (e.g., point cloud clustering)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin System’s technical connection** is structured as a **hybrid network architecture** comprising:
  - **Intra-entity communication** (within the Digital Twin Entity) via **ROS2**, a middleware framework enabling modular software components to exchange messages (e.g., sensor data, control commands) with **low-latency requirements** (≤100 ms from vehicle to Digital Twin Entity).
  - **Inter-entity communication** (Physical Twin ↔ Digital Twin ↔ User Entity) over **Wi-Fi using UDP**, ensuring lightweight, high-throughput data transfer for real-time applications.
  The **network topology** is hierarchical, with the **Physical Twin Entity** (sensors/actuators) feeding data to the **Digital Twin Entity** (processing/visualization), which interfaces with the **User Entity** (e.g., virtual cockpit). **Bandwidth and latency** are critical, with the system enforcing a **maximum end-to-end latency of 100 ms** for vehicle-to-DT data flows. **Reliability mechanisms** include redundant sensor streams (e.g., LiDAR + camera fusion implied by Fig. 3) and **data serialization/translation** via the **Data Translation FE**, which aligns message types and performs unit conversions. **Security protocols** are not explicitly detailed, though the use of **UDP over Wi-Fi** suggests prioritization of speed over encryption; no mention of redundancy mechanisms (e.g., failover nodes) or encryption standards (e.g., TLS) is provided."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin System’s hosting and deployment** infrastructure is **edge/on-premise centric**, with the **Physical Twin Entity** reflecting the inherent resources of the twinned system (e.g., compute, sensors, actuators). The **Digital Twin Entity** leverages:
  - **Local compute resources** (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson platforms implied by real-time processing requirements),
  - **On-premise data storage** via a **SQL database** (for object tracks and ego-vehicle state),
  - **No cloud dependency**, as all processing and storage are confined to the vehicle’s embedded systems or nearby edge devices.
  **Scalability mechanisms** are not explicitly described, though the modular ROS2-based architecture suggests potential for **horizontal scaling** (e.g., adding nodes for additional sensors). **Availability/reliability assurance** relies on:
  - **Real-time data processing** (e.g., LiDAR clustering, ACC logic) with no mention of backup systems or restart policies,
  - **Local redundancy** (e.g., sensor fusion from multiple sources),
  - **Deterministic latency** (≤100 ms) enforced via ROS2 and UDP/Wi-Fi. The deployment model is **monolithic for the Physical Twin** (embedded sensors/actuators) and **modular for the Digital Twin** (software components deployed as ROS2 nodes)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system complies with the **ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which establishes:
  - **Entity-based architecture**: A standardized framework where **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)** (e.g., equipment, facilities, products) are digitally represented via **Digital Twin Entities**, with **Data Collection and Device Control Entities** managing real-time interactions and **User Entities** providing interfaces for monitoring/control.
  - **Interoperability standards**: The architecture supports modularity and interoperability, though no specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MTConnect) are mandated beyond the reference model. The **Functional Entities (FEs)** within each entity may leverage industry-standard communication protocols (e.g., OPC UA) for data exchange, as implied by the lack of prescribed implementation constraints.
  - **Certification/compliance frameworks**: Not explicitly detailed in the provided context, though adherence to ISO 23247 ensures alignment with manufacturing digital twin best practices. The standard does not reference additional certification requirements beyond its own framework."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **ISO 15622:2018** for **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) performance requirements**, ensuring compliance with standardized safety-critical operational constraints such as dynamic response thresholds and environmental adaptability. The system incorporates **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) service requirements** as defined in **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**, including a **maximum network latency of 100 ms** for real-time communication, critical for safety-critical applications like collision avoidance. However, the provided documents do not specify explicit **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, or **authentication systems**. The system lacks documented evidence of **threat mitigation strategies**, **fail-safe mechanisms**, or **risk assessment approaches** beyond the referenced standards. **ISO 26262-1:2018** is not mentioned in the provided sources, and no functional safety framework is explicitly described."
]
